narration: The dealer shows you $611 a month to lease versus $738 to buy. That lower number just triggered something in your brain — and the dealer knows exactly what comes next.
visual: Dark background, SVG car, two price tags: $611/mo in orange vs $738/mo in grey, animated arrow pointing to lower number, 'YOUR BRAIN PICKS THE LOWER ONE' text reveal

narration: 36 months later, you hand back the keys. You've paid $21,996 into that lease — and you walk away with exactly zero dollars in equity. You financed someone else's depreciation.
visual: Light background, running counter filling to $21,996, empty wallet SVG revealing, large red $0 equity springs in

narration: Meanwhile, the buyer paid $738 a month for those same 36 months. That's $4,572 more — but they now own a car worth $20,000. That's what equity looks like.
visual: Dark background, two columns: LEASE shows $21,996 paid, $0 owned in red; BUY shows $26,568 paid, $20K car value in green; THAT'S CALLED EQUITY label

narration: Here's where the trap locks in. You get another new car every three years, paying $611 forever. Three leases, nine years: you've paid $66,000 and owned nothing, ever.
visual: Light background, three lease cycle boxes spring in one by one summing $21,996 each, total $65,988 reveals in red, 'AND YOU OWN NOTHING' below

narration: And that's before the fine print kicks in. Go over 12,000 miles and you owe 30 cents per mile. The average American drives 15,000 — that's $900 in hidden fees every single year.
visual: Dark background, odometer gauge needle sweeping past 12K limit into red zone, $0.30/mile overage fee appears, $900 hidden fee per year reveal

narration: The play? Buy a reliable used car, drive it to 150,000 miles, and invest the difference. Over a decade, that's $22,000 back in your pocket — follow for the math they hope you never see.
visual: Light background, used car SVG with 150K miles badge, three tips reveal in sequence, $22,000 savings callout springs in, follow CTA text
